chapter_title.ar: بَابُ الْمُتَمَتِّعِ تَعْرِضُ لَهُ الْحَاجَةُ خَارِجاً مِنْ مَكَّةَ بَعْدَ إِحْلَالِهِ
chapter_title.en: Chapter 148 – A need presents itself to the performer of Tumatto, outside of Makkah, after his being free from Ihraam

gradings: Allamah Baqir al-Majlisi: حسن - Mir‘at al ‘Uqul Fi Sharh Akhbar Al al Rasul (8/87)
Allamah Baqir al-Majlisi: حسن - Mir‘at al ‘Uqul Fi Sharh Akhbar Al al Rasul (8/87)
text: سَأَلْتُ أَبَا عَبْدِ اللَّهِ ( عليه السلام ) عَنِ الرَّجُلِ يَتَمَتَّعُ بِالْعُمْرَةِ إِلَى الْحَجِّ يُرِيدُ الْخُرُوجَ إِلَى الطَّائِفِ قَالَ يُهِلُّ بِالْحَجِّ مِنْ مَكَّةَ وَ مَا أُحِبُّ لَهُ أَنْ يَخْرُجَ مِنْهَا إِلَّا مُحْرِماً وَ لَا يَتَجَاوَزُ الطَّائِفَ إِنَّهَا قَرِيبَةٌ مِنْ مَكَّةَ .

en.hubeali: Ali Bin Ibrahim, from his father, from Ibn Abu Umeyr, from Hammad, from Al Halby who said,

‘I asked Abu Abdullahasws about the man performing Tumatto with the Umra to the Hajj, intending the going out to Al-Taif. Heasws said: ‘He should begin with the Hajj from Makkah, and Iasws do no love for him that he should go out from it unless he is in Ihraam, and he should not exceed Al-Taif, it is nearby from Makkah’.
en.sarwar: 3. Ali ibn Ibrahim has narrated from his father from ibn abu ‘Umayr from Hammad from al-Halabiy who has said the following: “I once asked abu ‘Abd Allah, ‘Alayhi al-Salam, about a man who is for al-Hajj al-Tamattu‘ (advantageous) but wants to go to Ta’if. He (the Imam) said, ‘He must commence al-Hajj from Makkah and I do not like for him to go out without Ihram. He must not go beyond Ta’if; it is not far from Makkah.’”
